title: panicum crus-galli subsp. hostii
intro: Panicum crus-galli subsp. hostii, also known as Japanese millet or wild proso millet, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Japanese millet is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in combination with other herbs to create various herbal preparations.
